summary: Global markets are displaying a cautiously optimistic mood, with U.S. equities continuing their 2025 rally that has already minted nearly one million new millionaires according to a fresh UBS report, though gains remain concentrated among top earners. The dominant macro catalyst is the geopolitical realignment in the Middle East following the death of Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei, where a U.S.-Iran deal has prompted Kuwait to sharply boost crude production in June, while UBS has already cut its 2026–2027 oil price forecasts as Strait of Hormuz flows recover. On the sector front, GE Vernova is emerging as a standout industrial beneficiary of AI-driven power demand beyond its gas turbines, while Jeff Bezos' family office backing five AI startups in June underscores continued aggressive capital deployment into the artificial intelligence space. The principal risk factors commanding investor attention include potential military escalation around Khamenei's funeral processions — with Iran explicitly warning the U.S. and Israel against strikes — ongoing regional instability highlighted by the Damascus café bombing, and demand-side weakness in oil markets evidenced by China's Hengli scrapping Mideast and West African crude purchases while cutting output. In the short term, markets appear biased toward modest gains as oil supply pressures ease and AI-related investment activity stays robust, but any miscalculation in the Middle East during the funeral period could rapidly inject volatility into energy prices and risk assets.
